message:
feat(core): add bounded statement-reuse trace for compile-governance lane telemetry

Adds a new connection-local, bounded trace buffer that records
per-lane statement-reuse observations so compile-governance
tooling can distinguish "same-lane reuse" (good: cache is hot
for the thread that compiled the statement) from "cross-lane /
cross-node churn" (warning: cache line is bouncing between
workers or hosts). The trace is advisory-only — it never
changes compile / prepare / execute behavior, it just records
enough context for later analysis.

## New types

- `STATEMENT_REUSE_TRACE_CAPACITY = 1_024` — hard cap on the
  ring buffer so a long-running connection cannot grow the
  trace unboundedly.
- `StatementReuseLaneContext { worker_id, node_id }` — captures
  the "who is compiling this" lane identity. Populated from
  `WORKER_ID` / `FSQLITE_WORKER_ID` env vars with a fallback
  chain through the thread name or `std::thread::current().id()`
  Debug representation, and `NODE_ID` / `FSQLITE_NODE_ID` /
  `HOSTNAME` with a fallback to `"(local)"`.
- `statement_reuse_lane_context()` — factory that pulls the
  current lane context. Under `#[cfg(test)]` it additionally
  consults a `thread_local! FSQLITE_STATEMENT_REUSE_LANE_CONTEXT_OVERRIDE`
  slot so unit tests can deterministically inject synthetic
  lane identities without poking process-global env vars.
- `StatementReuseLaneLocality` — enum with four variants:
  `FirstObservation`, `SameLane`, `CrossLane`, `CrossNode`.
  A const `as_str()` helper returns the snake_case label for
  log emission.
- `StatementReuseTraceKey { cache_kind: &'static str,
  statement_fingerprint: String }` — dedup key for "have I
  seen this statement before on any lane?".
- `StatementReuseLastSeen { cache_sequence, worker_id,
  node_id }` — the previous observation of a given trace key,
  used to compute reuse distance and lane locality.
- `StatementReuseTraceEntry` — the ring-buffer entry itself:
  cache_kind, statement_fingerprint, cache_sequence, cache_hit,
  reuse_distance (Option<u64>, None for first observation),
  lane_locality, worker_id, node_id, and the previous
  worker_id / node_id (Option<String> each) so downstream
  analysis can chart which lanes pass a statement between each
  other.
- `StatementReuseTraceState { next_sequence_by_cache_kind,
  last_seen, recent }` — the full mutable state backing the
  trace. Kept in a single `RefCell` on `Connection`.

## record() algorithm

`StatementReuseTraceState::record(cache_kind,
statement_fingerprint, cache_hit, lane) -> StatementReuseTraceEntry`:

1. Bump `next_sequence_by_cache_kind[cache_kind]` by one
   (saturating) to assign this observation a cache-kind-local
   monotonic sequence.
2. Build a `StatementReuseTraceKey` from the cache kind and
   fingerprint, then `last_seen.insert` the current
   `StatementReuseLastSeen` and capture the previous one (if
   any).
3. If there was a previous observation, compute
   `lane_locality`: `CrossNode` when `previous.node_id !=
   lane.node_id`, `CrossLane` when the workers differ, and
   `SameLane` when both match. Also compute
   `reuse_distance = cache_sequence - previous.cache_sequence
   - 1` so the distance measures "how many other statements
   of this cache kind happened between this reuse and the
   previous one". Both subtractions are saturating.
4. If there was no previous observation, record
   `reuse_distance = None`, `lane_locality = FirstObservation`,
   and leave the previous worker/node ids as `None`.
5. Push the resulting `StatementReuseTraceEntry` onto the
   `recent` `VecDeque`. If the ring buffer is already at
   `STATEMENT_REUSE_TRACE_CAPACITY`, `pop_front()` the oldest
   entry first.
6. Return the freshly recorded entry so the call site can
   emit its own telemetry if desired.

`clear()` empties all three sub-maps / deques so
`reset_statement_reuse_trace()` can bring the connection back
to a clean slate between test cases.

## Connection integration

`Connection` gains a new field:
`statement_reuse_trace: RefCell<StatementReuseTraceState>`,
initialized to `StatementReuseTraceState::default()` in both
`Connection` constructors. Two new `#[cfg(test)]` helpers
(`statement_reuse_trace_snapshot()` and
`reset_statement_reuse_trace()`) let unit tests read and
reset the bounded trace without exposing it publicly. The
production read path for the trace is not wired up yet —
this commit is intentionally additive so the capture side
can be landed independently from the consumer side. A follow-
up commit will plug record() into the compile / prepare-cache
call sites that produce the observations.

## Imports

`std::collections::VecDeque` is added to the existing
collections import block for the ring buffer.
